title: mentzelia humilis
intro: Mentzelia humilis, also known as low Indian plum or low blazing star, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its white or yellowish flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Mentzelia humilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities. Herbal preparations of Mentzelia humilis can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by making a tincture, and are said to be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and infections.
